keyPoints: Frame the core question: diversity in AI-enabled feeds and its impact on exposure diversity and belonging over multiple months.
Experimental design blueprint: four-arm cluster-randomized field design (baseline, diversity-forward, hybrid, peacebuilder) over 6-12 months.
Measurement strategy: primary outcomes of exposure diversity and belonging, with monthly measurement and cross-community interaction metrics.
Safeguards and ethics: conflict-aware design to prevent polarization and harassment, plus moderation and safety protocols.
Practical guidance: translate findings into ranking heuristics, explicit diversity targets, and cross-community dialogue features for real-world apps.
Thematic alignment: tie to Sacred Struggle thesis—friction, deliberate difficulty, and long-horizon skill development as pathways to taste, judgment, and connection.
